<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/deploy-file-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 258 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--sling:deploy-file"></a>

# sling:deploy-file

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:deploy-file

**Deprecated**:

Use the OBR goals from the [maven-bundle-plugin](https://felix.apache.org/documentation/_attachments/components/bundle-plugin/index.html) instead.

**Description**:

Deploy a JAR representing an OSGi Bundle to a Sling OSGi Bundle Repository. This method posts the bundle referenced via `sling.file` to an OSGi Bundle Repository accepting the bundle. The plugin uses a *multipart/format-data* POST request to just post the file to the URL configured in the `obr` property.

**Attributes**:

- The goal is not marked as thread-safe and thus does not support parallel builds.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<obr>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--obr) | `String` | `-` | The URL to the OSGi Bundle repository to which the bundle is posted, e.g. `http://obr.sample.com` **User Property**: `obr` |
| [`<slingUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--slingurl) | `URI` | `-` | The URL of the running Sling instance. The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment. For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets. **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console` **User Property**: `sling.url` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<bundleFileName>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--bundlefilename) | `String` | `-` | The name of the generated JAR file. **User Property**: `sling.file` |
| [`<failOnError>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--failonerror) | `boolean` | `-` | Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code. **Default**: `true` **User Property**: `sling.failOnError` |
| [`<httpConnectTimeoutSec>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec) | `int` | `3.0.0` | HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation). **Default**: `10` **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec` |
| [`<httpResponseTimeoutSec>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec) | `int` | `3.0.0` | HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint. **Default**: `60` **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec` |
| [`<password>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--password) | `String` | `-` | The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance. **Default**: `admin` **User Property**: `sling.password` |
| [`<slingConsoleUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--slingconsoleurl) | `URI` | `-` | The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used. **User Property**: `sling.console.url` |
| [`<slingUrlSuffix>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--slingurlsuffix) | `String` | `-` | An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods. **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix` |
| [`<user>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--user) | `String` | `-` | The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance. **Default**: `admin` **User Property**: `sling.user` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--bundlefilename"></a>

### <bundleFileName>

The name of the generated JAR file.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.file`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--failonerror"></a>

### <failOnError>

Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.failOnError`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpConnectTimeoutSec>

HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation).

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `10`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpResponseTimeoutSec>

HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `60`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--obr"></a>

### <obr>

The URL to the OSGi Bundle repository to which the bundle is posted, e.g. `http://obr.sample.com`

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `obr`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--password"></a>

### <password>

The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.password`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--slingconsoleurl"></a>

### <slingConsoleUrl>

The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.console.url`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--slingurl"></a>

### <slingUrl>

The URL of the running Sling instance.

The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment.

For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `sling.url`
- **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--slingurlsuffix"></a>

### <slingUrlSuffix>

An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo--user"></a>

### <user>

The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.user`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/deploy-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 259 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--sling:deploy"></a>

# sling:deploy

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:deploy

**Deprecated**:

Use the OBR goals from the [maven-bundle-plugin](https://felix.apache.org/documentation/_attachments/components/bundle-plugin/index.html) instead.

**Description**:

Deploy a JAR representing an OSGi Bundle to a Sling OSGi Bundle Repository. This method posts the bundle built by maven to the OSGi Bundle Repository. The plugin uses a *multipart/format-data* POST request to just post the file to the URL configured in the `obr` property.

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- The goal is not marked as thread-safe and thus does not support parallel builds.
- Binds by default to the [lifecycle phase](https://maven.apache.org/ref/current/maven-core/lifecycles.html): `deploy`.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<jarName>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--jarname) | `String` | `-` | The name of the generated JAR file. **User Property**: `project.build.finalName` **Alias**: `jarName` |
| [`<obr>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--obr) | `String` | `-` | The URL to the OSGi Bundle repository to which the bundle is posted, e.g. `http://obr.sample.com` **User Property**: `obr` |
| [`<skip>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--skip) | `boolean` | `-` | Whether to skip this step even though it has been configured in the project to be executed. This property may be set by the `sling.deploy.skip` comparable to the `maven.test.skip` property to prevent running the unit tests. **Default**: `false` **User Property**: `sling.deploy.skip` |
| [`<slingUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--slingurl) | `URI` | `-` | The URL of the running Sling instance. The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment. For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets. **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console` **User Property**: `sling.url` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<failOnError>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--failonerror) | `boolean` | `-` | Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code. **Default**: `true` **User Property**: `sling.failOnError` |
| [`<httpConnectTimeoutSec>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec) | `int` | `3.0.0` | HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation). **Default**: `10` **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec` |
| [`<httpResponseTimeoutSec>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec) | `int` | `3.0.0` | HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint. **Default**: `60` **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec` |
| [`<password>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--password) | `String` | `-` | The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance. **Default**: `admin` **User Property**: `sling.password` |
| [`<slingConsoleUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--slingconsoleurl) | `URI` | `-` | The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used. **User Property**: `sling.console.url` |
| [`<slingUrlSuffix>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--slingurlsuffix) | `String` | `-` | An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods. **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix` |
| [`<user>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--user) | `String` | `-` | The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance. **Default**: `admin` **User Property**: `sling.user` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--failonerror"></a>

### <failOnError>

Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.failOnError`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpConnectTimeoutSec>

HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation).

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `10`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpResponseTimeoutSec>

HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `60`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--jarname"></a>

### <jarName>

The name of the generated JAR file.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `project.build.finalName`
- **Alias**: `jarName`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--obr"></a>

### <obr>

The URL to the OSGi Bundle repository to which the bundle is posted, e.g. `http://obr.sample.com`

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `obr`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--password"></a>

### <password>

The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.password`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--skip"></a>

### <skip>

Whether to skip this step even though it has been configured in the project to be executed. This property may be set by the `sling.deploy.skip` comparable to the `maven.test.skip` property to prevent running the unit tests.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `sling.deploy.skip`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--slingconsoleurl"></a>

### <slingConsoleUrl>

The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.console.url`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--slingurl"></a>

### <slingUrl>

The URL of the running Sling instance.

The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment.

For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `sling.url`
- **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--slingurlsuffix"></a>

### <slingUrlSuffix>

An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo--user"></a>

### <user>

The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.user`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/fsmount-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 260 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--sling:fsmount"></a>

# sling:fsmount

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:fsmount

**Description**:

Create OSGi configurations for the [Apache Sling File System Resource Provider](#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource). In case a bundle file is supplied via `bundleFileName` the configuration for its [initial content](#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading) is created. Otherwise it tries to detect a FileVault content package layout starting at `fileVaultJcrRootFile` or the project's resource directories and potentially creates a configuration for each path in the package's `filter.xml`.

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- The goal is not marked as thread-safe and thus does not support parallel builds.
- Since version: `2.2.0`.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<slingUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--slingurl) | `URI` | `-` | The URL of the running Sling instance. The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment. For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets. **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console` **User Property**: `sling.url` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

This is the default. Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points to the Felix Web Console in that case.</li>
<li><strong>WebDAV</strong>, uses <a href="#documentation-development-repository-based-development"> WebDAV</a> for deployment (HTTP PUT). Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points to the entry path of the Sling WebDAV bundle (defaults to <code>/dav/default</code> in the Sling starter). Issues a HTTP Delete for the uninstall goal.</li>
<li><strong>SlingPostServlet</strong>, uses the <a href="#documentation-bundles-manipulating-content-the-slingpostservlet-servlets-post">Sling Post Servlet</a> for deployment (HTTP POST). Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points a path which is handled by the Sling POST Servlet (usually below regular Sling root URL).</li>

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--bundlefilename"></a>

### <bundleFileName>

The path of the bundle file whose Sling Initial Content should be mounted.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.file`
- **Default**: `${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--deployfsresourcebundle"></a>

### <deployFsResourceBundle>

Deploy `org.apache.sling.fsresource` to Sling instance bundle when it is not deployed already.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Since**: `2.3.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--deployfsresourcebundleprerequisites"></a>

### <deployFsResourceBundlePrerequisites>

Bundles that have to be installed as prerequisites to execute this goal. With multiple entries in the list different bundles with different preconditions can be defined.
**Default value is:**:

```

<deployFsResourceBundlePrerequisites>
  <bundlePrerequisite>
    <bundles>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
        <artifactId>org.apache.sling.fsresource</artifactId>
        <version>2.3.0</version>
      </bundle>
    </bundles>
    <preconditions>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
        <artifactId>org.apache.sling.commons.johnzon</artifactId>
        <version>2.0.0</version>
      </bundle>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
        <artifactId>org.apache.sling.api</artifactId>
        <version>2.25.4</version>
      </bundle>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.commons</groupId>
        <artifactId>commons-collections4</artifactId>
        <version>4.1</version>
        <symbolicName>org.apache.commons.collections4</symbolicName>
      </bundle>
    </preconditions>
  </bundlePrerequisite>
  <bundlePrerequisite>
    <bundles>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
        <artifactId>org.apache.sling.commons.johnzon</artifactId>
        <version>1.2.6</version>
      </bundle>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.commons</groupId>
        <artifactId>commons-collections4</artifactId>
        <version>4.1</version>
        <symbolicName>org.apache.commons.collections4</symbolicName>
      </bundle>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
        <artifactId>org.apache.sling.fsresource</artifactId>
        <version>2.2.0</version>
      </bundle>
    </bundles>
    <preconditions>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
        <artifactId>org.apache.sling.resourceresolver</artifactId>
        <version>1.5.18</version>
      </bundle>
    </preconditions>
  </bundlePrerequisite>
  <bundlePrerequisite>
    <bundles>
      <bundle>
        <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
        <artifactId>org.apache.sling.fsresource</artifactId>
        <version>1.4.8</version>
      </bundle>
    </bundles>
  </bundlePrerequisite>
</deployFsResourceBundlePrerequisites>
```

- **Type**: `java.util.List<org.apache.sling.maven.bundlesupport.BundlePrerequisite>`
- **Since**: `2.3.0`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--deploymentmethod"></a>

### <deploymentMethod>

Bundle deployment method. One of the following three values are allowed

1. **WebConsole**, uses the  [Felix Web Console REST API](http://felix.apache.org/documentation/subprojects/apache-felix-web-console/web-console-restful-api.html#post-requests) for deployment (HTTP POST). This is the default. Make sure that `slingUrl` points to the Felix Web Console in that case.
2. **WebDAV**, uses  [WebDAV](#documentation-development-repository-based-development) for deployment (HTTP PUT). Make sure that `slingUrl` points to the entry path of the Sling WebDAV bundle (defaults to `/dav/default` in the Sling starter). Issues a HTTP Delete for the uninstall goal.
3. **SlingPostServlet**, uses the [Sling Post Servlet](#documentation-bundles-manipulating-content-the-slingpostservlet-servlets-post) for deployment (HTTP POST). Make sure that `slingUrl` points a path which is handled by the Sling POST Servlet (usually below regular Sling root URL).

For more details refer to [Bundle Installation](#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation).

- **Type**: `org.apache.sling.maven.bundlesupport.deploy.BundleDeploymentMethod`
- **Since**: `2.3.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.deploy.method`
- **Default**: `WebConsole`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--failonerror"></a>

### <failOnError>

Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.failOnError`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--filevaultfilterxmlfile"></a>

### <fileVaultFilterXmlFile>

Path to META-INF/vault/filter.xml when using FileVault XML filesystem layout.

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.filevault.filterxml.file`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--filevaultjcrrootfile"></a>

### <fileVaultJcrRootFile>

FileVault filesystem layout content root folder.

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.filevault.jcr_root.file`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpConnectTimeoutSec>

HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation).

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `10`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpResponseTimeoutSec>

HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `60`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--password"></a>

### <password>

The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.password`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--skip"></a>

### <skip>

If set to `true` Sling File System Resource mount/unmount commands are ignored.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.fsmount.skip`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--slingconsoleurl"></a>

### <slingConsoleUrl>

The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.console.url`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--slingurl"></a>

### <slingUrl>

The URL of the running Sling instance.

The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment.

For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `sling.url`
- **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--slingurlsuffix"></a>

### <slingUrlSuffix>

An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo--user"></a>

### <user>

The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.user`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/fsunmount-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 261 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--sling:fsunmount"></a>

# sling:fsunmount

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:fsunmount

**Description**:

Remove OSGi configurations for the [Apache Sling File System Resource Provider](#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource). In case a bundle file is supplied via `bundleFileName` the configuration for its [initial content](#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading) is created. Otherwise it tries to detect a FileVault content package layout starting at `fileVaultJcrRootFile` or the project's resource directories and potentially creates a configuration for each path in the package's `filter.xml`.

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- The goal is not marked as thread-safe and thus does not support parallel builds.
- Since version: `2.2.0`.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<slingUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--slingurl) | `URI` | `-` | The URL of the running Sling instance. The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment. For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets. **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console` **User Property**: `sling.url` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<bundleFileName>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--bundlefilename) | `String` | `-` | The path of the bundle file whose Sling Initial Content should be mounted. **Default**: `${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar` **User Property**: `sling.file` |
| [`<failOnError>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--failonerror) | `boolean` | `-` | Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code. **Default**: `true` **User Property**: `sling.failOnError` |
| [`<fileVaultFilterXmlFile>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--filevaultfilterxmlfile) | `File` | `-` | Path to META-INF/vault/filter.xml when using FileVault XML filesystem layout. **User Property**: `sling.filevault.filterxml.file` |
| [`<fileVaultJcrRootFile>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--filevaultjcrrootfile) | `File` | `-` | FileVault filesystem layout content root folder. **User Property**: `sling.filevault.jcr_root.file` |
| [`<httpConnectTimeoutSec>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec) | `int` | `3.0.0` | HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation). **Default**: `10` **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec` |
| [`<httpResponseTimeoutSec>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec) | `int` | `3.0.0` | HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint. **Default**: `60` **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec` |
| [`<password>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--password) | `String` | `-` | The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance. **Default**: `admin` **User Property**: `sling.password` |
| [`<skip>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--skip) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` Sling File System Resource mount/unmount commands are ignored. **Default**: `false` **User Property**: `sling.fsmount.skip` |
| [`<slingConsoleUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--slingconsoleurl) | `URI` | `-` | The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used. **User Property**: `sling.console.url` |
| [`<slingUrlSuffix>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--slingurlsuffix) | `String` | `-` | An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods. **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix` |
| [`<user>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--user) | `String` | `-` | The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance. **Default**: `admin` **User Property**: `sling.user` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--bundlefilename"></a>

### <bundleFileName>

The path of the bundle file whose Sling Initial Content should be mounted.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.file`
- **Default**: `${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--failonerror"></a>

### <failOnError>

Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.failOnError`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--filevaultfilterxmlfile"></a>

### <fileVaultFilterXmlFile>

Path to META-INF/vault/filter.xml when using FileVault XML filesystem layout.

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.filevault.filterxml.file`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--filevaultjcrrootfile"></a>

### <fileVaultJcrRootFile>

FileVault filesystem layout content root folder.

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.filevault.jcr_root.file`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpConnectTimeoutSec>

HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation).

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `10`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpResponseTimeoutSec>

HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `60`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--password"></a>

### <password>

The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.password`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--skip"></a>

### <skip>

If set to `true` Sling File System Resource mount/unmount commands are ignored.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.fsmount.skip`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--slingconsoleurl"></a>

### <slingConsoleUrl>

The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.console.url`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--slingurl"></a>

### <slingUrl>

The URL of the running Sling instance.

The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment.

For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `sling.url`
- **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--slingurlsuffix"></a>

### <slingUrlSuffix>

An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo--user"></a>

### <user>

The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.user`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/generate-adapter-metadata-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 262 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--sling:generate-adapter-metadata"></a>

# sling:generate-adapter-metadata

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:generate-adapter-metadata

**Description**:

Build [adapter metadata (JSON)](#documentation-the-sling-engine-adapters--implementing-adaptable) for the Web Console Plugin at `/system/console/status-adapters` and `/system/console/adapters` from classes annotated with [adapter annotations](https://github.com/apache/sling-adapter-annotations).

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- Requires dependency resolution of artifacts in scope: `compile`.
- The goal is thread-safe and supports parallel builds.
- Binds by default to the [lifecycle phase](https://maven.apache.org/ref/current/maven-core/lifecycles.html): `process-classes`.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<buildOutputDirectory>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--buildoutputdirectory) | `File` | `-` | The directory which to check for classes with adapter metadata annotations. **Default**: `${project.build.outputDirectory}` **User Property**: `adapter.build.output.directory` |
| [`<fileName>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--filename) | `String` | `-` | Name of the generated descriptor file (relative to `outputDirectory`). **Default**: `SLING-INF/adapters.json` **User Property**: `adapter.descriptor.name` |
| [`<outputDirectory>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--outputdirectory) | `File` | `-` | The output directory in which to emit the descriptor file with name `fileName`. **Default**: `${project.build.outputDirectory}` **User Property**: `adapter.output.directory` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--buildoutputdirectory"></a>

### <buildOutputDirectory>

The directory which to check for classes with adapter metadata annotations.

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `adapter.build.output.directory`
- **Default**: `${project.build.outputDirectory}`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--filename"></a>

### <fileName>

Name of the generated descriptor file (relative to `outputDirectory`).

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `adapter.descriptor.name`
- **Default**: `SLING-INF/adapters.json`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo--outputdirectory"></a>

### <outputDirectory>

The output directory in which to emit the descriptor file with name `fileName`.

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `adapter.output.directory`
- **Default**: `${project.build.outputDirectory}`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/help-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 263 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--sling:help"></a>

# sling:help

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:help

**Description**:

Display help information on sling-maven-plugin.
Call `mvn sling:help -Ddetail=true -Dgoal=<goal-name>` to display parameter details.

**Attributes**:

- The goal is thread-safe and supports parallel builds.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<detail>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--detail) | `boolean` | `-` | If `true`, display all settable properties for each goal. **Default**: `false` **User Property**: `detail` |
| [`<goal>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--goal) | `String` | `-` | The name of the goal for which to show help. If unspecified, all goals will be displayed. **User Property**: `goal` |
| [`<indentSize>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--indentsize) | `int` | `-` | The number of spaces per indentation level, should be positive. **Default**: `2` **User Property**: `indentSize` |
| [`<lineLength>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--linelength) | `int` | `-` | The maximum length of a display line, should be positive. **Default**: `80` **User Property**: `lineLength` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--detail"></a>

### <detail>

If `true`, display all settable properties for each goal.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `detail`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--goal"></a>

### <goal>

The name of the goal for which to show help. If unspecified, all goals will be displayed.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `goal`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--indentsize"></a>

### <indentSize>

The number of spaces per indentation level, should be positive.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `indentSize`
- **Default**: `2`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo--linelength"></a>

### <lineLength>

The maximum length of a display line, should be positive.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `lineLength`
- **Default**: `80`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/ -->

<!-- page_index: 264 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin--sling-maven-plugin"></a>

# Sling Maven Plugin

The Sling Maven Plugin provides a number of goals which may be of help while developing bundles for Sling.

See [Goals](#components-sling-maven-plugin-plugin-info) for a list of supported goals.

Details about the different approaches for installing bundles are explained in [Bundle Installation](#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation).

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/install-file-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 265 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--sling:install-file"></a>

# sling:install-file

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:install-file

**Description**:

Install an OSGi bundle from a given file path or Maven coordinates (resolved from the repository) to a running Sling instance. One of the following parameter sets are used to determine the bundle to install (evaluated in the given order):

- `groupId`, `artifactId`, `version`, and optionally `classifier` and `packaging`
- `artifact`
- `bundleFileName`

To install a bundle which has been built from the current Maven project rather use goal install. For details refer to [Bundle Installation](#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation).

**Attributes**:

- The goal is not marked as thread-safe and thus does not support parallel builds.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<slingUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--slingurl) | `URI` | `-` | The URL of the running Sling instance. The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment. For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets. **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console` **User Property**: `sling.url` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

<table class="table table-striped">
<tr>
<th>Name</th>
<th>Type</th>
<th>Since</th>
<th>Description</th></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--artifact"><code>&lt;artifact&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>A string of the form <code>groupId:artifactId:version[:packaging[:classifier]]</code>. Takes precedence over <code>bundleFileName</code>.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.artifact</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--artifactid"><code>&lt;artifactId&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The artifactId of the artifact to install. Takes precedence over <code>artifact</code> and <code>bundleFileName</code>.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.artifactId</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--bundlefilename"><code>&lt;bundleFileName&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>File</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The path of the bundle file to install. Is only effective if artifact is not determined via some other way (Maven coordinates).
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.file</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--bundlestart"><code>&lt;bundleStart&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Whether to start the uploaded bundle or not. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>true</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.bundle.start</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--bundlestartlevel"><code>&lt;bundleStartLevel&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The start level to set on the installed bundle. If the bundle is already installed and therefore is only updated this parameter is ignored. The parameter is also ignored if the running Sling instance has no StartLevel service (which is unusual actually). Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>20</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.bundle.startlevel</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--classifier"><code>&lt;classifier&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The classifier of the artifact to install
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.classifier</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--deploymentmethod"><code>&lt;deploymentMethod&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>BundleDeploymentMethod</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Bundle deployment method. One of the following three values are allowed
<ol>
<li><strong>WebConsole</strong>, uses the <a href="http://felix.apache.org/documentation/subprojects/apache-felix-web-console/web-console-restful-api.html#post-requests"> Felix Web Console REST API</a> for deployment (HTTP POST). This is the default. Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points to the Felix Web Console in that case.</li>
<li><strong>WebDAV</strong>, uses <a href="#documentation-development-repository-based-development"> WebDAV</a> for deployment (HTTP PUT). Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points to the entry path of the Sling WebDAV bundle (defaults to <code>/dav/default</code> in the Sling starter). Issues a HTTP Delete for the uninstall goal.</li>
<li><strong>SlingPostServlet</strong>, uses the <a href="#documentation-bundles-manipulating-content-the-slingpostservlet-servlets-post">Sling Post Servlet</a> for deployment (HTTP POST). Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points a path which is handled by the Sling POST Servlet (usually below regular Sling root URL).</li>
</ol> For more details refer to <a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation">Bundle Installation</a>. This has precedence over the deprecated parameter <code>usePut</code>. If nothing is set the default is either <code>WebConsole</code> or <code>WebDAV</code> (when <code>usePut</code> is <code>true</code>).
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.deploy.method</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--failonerror"><code>&lt;failOnError&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>true</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.failOnError</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--groupid"><code>&lt;groupId&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The groupId of the artifact to install. Takes precedence over <code>artifact</code> and <code>bundleFileName</code>.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.groupId</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"><code>&lt;httpConnectTimeoutSec&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>int</code></td>
<td><code>3.0.0</code></td>
<td>HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as <code>SSL</code> or <code>TLS</code> protocol negotiation).
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>10</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"><code>&lt;httpResponseTimeoutSec&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>int</code></td>
<td><code>3.0.0</code></td>
<td>HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>60</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--mimetype"><code>&lt;mimeType&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The content type / mime type used for WebDAV or Sling POST deployment.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>application/java-archive</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.mimeType</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--mountbyfs"><code>&lt;mountByFS&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Whether to add (for install)/remove (for uninstall) the mapping for the <a href="#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource">Apache Sling File System Resource Provider</a> for the <a href="#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading">bundle's initial content</a>. This parameter must not be <code>true</code> with bundles resolved from the Maven repository.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>false</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.mountByFS</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--packaging"><code>&lt;packaging&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The packaging of the artifact to install
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>jar</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.packaging</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--password"><code>&lt;password&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>admin</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.password</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--refreshpackages"><code>&lt;refreshPackages&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Whether to refresh the packages after installing the uploaded bundle. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>true</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.refreshPackages</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--slingconsoleurl"><code>&lt;slingConsoleUrl&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>URI</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.console.url</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--slingurlsuffix"><code>&lt;slingUrlSuffix&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>An optional url suffix which will be appended to the <code>sling.url</code> for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common <code>sling.url</code> in a parent POM (eg. <code>sling.url=http://localhost:8080</code> and <code>sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path</code>). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.urlSuffix</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--useput"><code>&lt;usePut&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>
<div><strong>Deprecated.</strong>
Use <code>deploymentMethod</code> instead.</div>
If a PUT via WebDAV should be used instead of the standard POST to the Felix Web Console. In the <code>uninstall</code> goal, a HTTP DELETE will be used.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>false</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.usePut</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--user"><code>&lt;user&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>admin</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.user</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--version"><code>&lt;version&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The version of the artifact to install. Takes precedence over <code>artifact</code> and <code>bundleFileName</code>.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.version</code></td></tr></table>

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--artifact"></a>

### <artifact>

A string of the form `groupId:artifactId:version[:packaging[:classifier]]`. Takes precedence over `bundleFileName`.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.artifact`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--artifactid"></a>

### <artifactId>

The artifactId of the artifact to install. Takes precedence over `artifact` and `bundleFileName`.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.artifactId`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--bundlefilename"></a>

### <bundleFileName>

The path of the bundle file to install. Is only effective if artifact is not determined via some other way (Maven coordinates).

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.file`
- **Default**: `${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--bundlestart"></a>

### <bundleStart>

Whether to start the uploaded bundle or not. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.bundle.start`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--bundlestartlevel"></a>

### <bundleStartLevel>

The start level to set on the installed bundle. If the bundle is already installed and therefore is only updated this parameter is ignored. The parameter is also ignored if the running Sling instance has no StartLevel service (which is unusual actually). Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.bundle.startlevel`
- **Default**: `20`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--classifier"></a>

### <classifier>

The classifier of the artifact to install

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.classifier`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--deploymentmethod"></a>

### <deploymentMethod>

Bundle deployment method. One of the following three values are allowed

1. **WebConsole**, uses the  [Felix Web Console REST API](http://felix.apache.org/documentation/subprojects/apache-felix-web-console/web-console-restful-api.html#post-requests) for deployment (HTTP POST). This is the default. Make sure that `slingUrl` points to the Felix Web Console in that case.
2. **WebDAV**, uses  [WebDAV](#documentation-development-repository-based-development) for deployment (HTTP PUT). Make sure that `slingUrl` points to the entry path of the Sling WebDAV bundle (defaults to `/dav/default` in the Sling starter). Issues a HTTP Delete for the uninstall goal.
3. **SlingPostServlet**, uses the [Sling Post Servlet](#documentation-bundles-manipulating-content-the-slingpostservlet-servlets-post) for deployment (HTTP POST). Make sure that `slingUrl` points a path which is handled by the Sling POST Servlet (usually below regular Sling root URL).

For more details refer to [Bundle Installation](#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation). This has precedence over the deprecated parameter `usePut`. If nothing is set the default is either `WebConsole` or `WebDAV` (when `usePut` is `true`).

- **Type**: `org.apache.sling.maven.bundlesupport.deploy.BundleDeploymentMethod`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.deploy.method`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--failonerror"></a>

### <failOnError>

Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.failOnError`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--groupid"></a>

### <groupId>

The groupId of the artifact to install. Takes precedence over `artifact` and `bundleFileName`.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.groupId`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpConnectTimeoutSec>

HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation).

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `10`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpResponseTimeoutSec>

HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `60`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--mimetype"></a>

### <mimeType>

The content type / mime type used for WebDAV or Sling POST deployment.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.mimeType`
- **Default**: `application/java-archive`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--mountbyfs"></a>

### <mountByFS>

Whether to add (for install)/remove (for uninstall) the mapping for the [Apache Sling File System Resource Provider](#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource) for the [bundle's initial content](#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading). This parameter must not be `true` with bundles resolved from the Maven repository.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.mountByFS`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--packaging"></a>

### <packaging>

The packaging of the artifact to install

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.packaging`
- **Default**: `jar`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--password"></a>

### <password>

The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.password`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--refreshpackages"></a>

### <refreshPackages>

Whether to refresh the packages after installing the uploaded bundle. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.refreshPackages`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--slingconsoleurl"></a>

### <slingConsoleUrl>

The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.console.url`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--slingurl"></a>

### <slingUrl>

The URL of the running Sling instance.

The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment.

For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `sling.url`
- **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--slingurlsuffix"></a>

### <slingUrlSuffix>

An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--useput"></a>

### <usePut>

**Deprecated.**
Use `deploymentMethod` instead.

If a PUT via WebDAV should be used instead of the standard POST to the Felix Web Console. In the `uninstall` goal, a HTTP DELETE will be used.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.usePut`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--user"></a>

### <user>

The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.user`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo--version"></a>

### <version>

The version of the artifact to install. Takes precedence over `artifact` and `bundleFileName`.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.version`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/install-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 266 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--sling:install"></a>

# sling:install

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:install

**Description**:

Install the project's artifact to a running Sling instance (in case it is an OSGi bundle). It uses the first valid OSGi bundle file for deployment from the primary artifact and all secondary ones. For details refer to [Bundle Installation](#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation). To install an arbitrary bundle not attached to the current Maven project use goal install-file.

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- The goal is not marked as thread-safe and thus does not support parallel builds.
- Binds by default to the [lifecycle phase](https://maven.apache.org/ref/current/maven-core/lifecycles.html): `install`.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<slingUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--slingurl) | `URI` | `-` | The URL of the running Sling instance. The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment. For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets. **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console` **User Property**: `sling.url` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters
