<td>Whether to skip this step even though it has been configured in the project to be executed.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>false</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.install.skip</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--slingconsoleurl"><code>&lt;slingConsoleUrl&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>URI</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.console.url</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--slingurlsuffix"><code>&lt;slingUrlSuffix&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>An optional url suffix which will be appended to the <code>sling.url</code> for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common <code>sling.url</code> in a parent POM (eg. <code>sling.url=http://localhost:8080</code> and <code>sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path</code>). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.urlSuffix</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--useput"><code>&lt;usePut&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>
<div><strong>Deprecated.</strong>
Use <code>deploymentMethod</code> instead.</div>
If a PUT via WebDAV should be used instead of the standard POST to the Felix Web Console. In the <code>uninstall</code> goal, a HTTP DELETE will be used.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>false</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.usePut</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--user"><code>&lt;user&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>admin</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.user</code></td></tr></table>

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--bundlestart"></a>

### <bundleStart>

Whether to start the uploaded bundle or not. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.bundle.start`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--bundlestartlevel"></a>

### <bundleStartLevel>

The start level to set on the installed bundle. If the bundle is already installed and therefore is only updated this parameter is ignored. The parameter is also ignored if the running Sling instance has no StartLevel service (which is unusual actually). Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.bundle.startlevel`
- **Default**: `20`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--deploymentmethod"></a>

### <deploymentMethod>

Bundle deployment method. One of the following three values are allowed

1. **WebConsole**, uses the  [Felix Web Console REST API](http://felix.apache.org/documentation/subprojects/apache-felix-web-console/web-console-restful-api.html#post-requests) for deployment (HTTP POST). This is the default. Make sure that `slingUrl` points to the Felix Web Console in that case.
2. **WebDAV**, uses  [WebDAV](#documentation-development-repository-based-development) for deployment (HTTP PUT). Make sure that `slingUrl` points to the entry path of the Sling WebDAV bundle (defaults to `/dav/default` in the Sling starter). Issues a HTTP Delete for the uninstall goal.
3. **SlingPostServlet**, uses the [Sling Post Servlet](#documentation-bundles-manipulating-content-the-slingpostservlet-servlets-post) for deployment (HTTP POST). Make sure that `slingUrl` points a path which is handled by the Sling POST Servlet (usually below regular Sling root URL).

For more details refer to [Bundle Installation](#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation). This has precedence over the deprecated parameter `usePut`. If nothing is set the default is either `WebConsole` or `WebDAV` (when `usePut` is `true`).

- **Type**: `org.apache.sling.maven.bundlesupport.deploy.BundleDeploymentMethod`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.deploy.method`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--failonerror"></a>

### <failOnError>

Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.failOnError`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpConnectTimeoutSec>

HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation).

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `10`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpResponseTimeoutSec>

HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `60`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--mimetype"></a>

### <mimeType>

The content type / mime type used for WebDAV or Sling POST deployment.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.mimeType`
- **Default**: `application/java-archive`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--mountbyfs"></a>

### <mountByFS>

Whether to add (for install)/remove (for uninstall) the mapping for the [Apache Sling File System Resource Provider](#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource) for the [bundle's initial content](#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading). This parameter must not be `true` with bundles resolved from the Maven repository.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.mountByFS`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--password"></a>

### <password>

The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.password`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--refreshpackages"></a>

### <refreshPackages>

Whether to refresh the packages after installing the uploaded bundle. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.refreshPackages`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--skip"></a>

### <skip>

Whether to skip this step even though it has been configured in the project to be executed.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.install.skip`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--slingconsoleurl"></a>

### <slingConsoleUrl>

The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.console.url`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--slingurl"></a>

### <slingUrl>

The URL of the running Sling instance.

The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment.

For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `sling.url`
- **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--slingurlsuffix"></a>

### <slingUrlSuffix>

An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--useput"></a>

### <usePut>

**Deprecated.**
Use `deploymentMethod` instead.

If a PUT via WebDAV should be used instead of the standard POST to the Felix Web Console. In the `uninstall` goal, a HTTP DELETE will be used.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.usePut`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo--user"></a>

### <user>

The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.user`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-mailing-lists"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/mailing-lists.html -->

<!-- page_index: 267 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-mailing-lists--project-mailing-lists"></a>

# Project Mailing Lists

These are the mailing lists that have been established for this project. For each list, there is a subscribe, unsubscribe, and an archive link.

| Name | Subscribe | Unsubscribe | Post | Archive | Other Archives |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Apache Sling Users List | [Subscribe](mailto:users-subscribe@sling.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:users-unsubscribe@sling.apache.org) | [Post](#project-information--mailing-lists) | [lists.apache.org](https://lists.apache.org/list.html?users@sling.apache.org) | [www.mail-archive.com](https://www.mail-archive.com/users@sling.apache.org/) |
| Apache Sling Development List | [Subscribe](mailto:dev-subscribe@sling.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:dev-unsubscribe@sling.apache.org) | [Post](#project-information--mailing-lists) | [lists.apache.org](https://lists.apache.org/list.html?dev@sling.apache.org) | [www.mail-archive.com](https://www.mail-archive.com/dev@sling.apache.org/) |
| Apache Sling Source Control List | [Subscribe](mailto:commits-subscribe@sling.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:commits-unsubscribe@sling.apache.org) | - | [mail-archives.apache.org](https://mail-archives.apache.org/mod_mbox/sling-commits/) | [sling-commits.markmail.org](https://sling-commits.markmail.org/) |
|  |  |  |  |  | [www.mail-archive.com](https://www.mail-archive.com/commits@sling.apache.org/) |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-plugin-info"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/plugin-info.html -->

<!-- page_index: 268 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-plugin-info--plugin-documentation"></a>

# Plugin Documentation

This report describes goals, parameters details, requirements and sample usage of this plugin.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-plugin-info--goals"></a>

## Goals

Goals available for this plugin:

<table class="table table-striped">
<tr>
<th>Goal</th>
<th>Description</th></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-mojo">sling:deploy</a></td>
<td>
<div><strong>Deprecated.</strong> Use the OBR goals from the <a href="https://felix.apache.org/documentation/_attachments/components/bundle-plugin/index.html">maven-bundle-plugin</a> instead.</div>
Deploy a JAR representing an OSGi Bundle to a Sling OSGi Bundle Repository. This method posts the bundle built by maven to the OSGi Bundle Repository. The plugin uses a <em>multipart/format-data</em> POST request to just post the file to the URL configured in the <code>obr</code> property.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-deploy-file-mojo">sling:deploy-file</a></td>
<td>
<div><strong>Deprecated.</strong> Use the OBR goals from the <a href="https://felix.apache.org/documentation/_attachments/components/bundle-plugin/index.html">maven-bundle-plugin</a> instead.</div>
Deploy a JAR representing an OSGi Bundle to a Sling OSGi Bundle Repository. This method posts the bundle referenced via <code>sling.file</code> to an OSGi Bundle Repository accepting the bundle. The plugin uses a <em>multipart/format-data</em> POST request to just post the file to the URL configured in the <code>obr</code> property.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsmount-mojo">sling:fsmount</a></td>
<td>Create OSGi configurations for the <a href="#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource">Apache Sling File System Resource Provider</a>. In case a bundle file is supplied via <code>bundleFileName</code> the configuration for its <a href="#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading">initial content</a> is created. Otherwise it tries to detect a FileVault content package layout starting at <code>fileVaultJcrRootFile</code> or the project's resource directories and potentially creates a configuration for each path in the package's <code>filter.xml</code>.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-fsunmount-mojo">sling:fsunmount</a></td>
<td>Remove OSGi configurations for the <a href="#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource">Apache Sling File System Resource Provider</a>. In case a bundle file is supplied via <code>bundleFileName</code> the configuration for its <a href="#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading">initial content</a> is created. Otherwise it tries to detect a FileVault content package layout starting at <code>fileVaultJcrRootFile</code> or the project's resource directories and potentially creates a configuration for each path in the package's <code>filter.xml</code>.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-generate-adapter-metadata-mojo">sling:generate-adapter-metadata</a></td>
<td>Build <a href="#documentation-the-sling-engine-adapters--implementing-adaptable">adapter metadata (JSON)</a> for the Web Console Plugin at <code>/system/console/status-adapters</code> and <code>/system/console/adapters</code> from classes annotated with <a href="https://github.com/apache/sling-adapter-annotations">adapter annotations</a>.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-help-mojo">sling:help</a></td>
<td>Display help information on sling-maven-plugin.

 Call <code>mvn sling:help -Ddetail=true -Dgoal=&lt;goal-name&gt;</code> to display parameter details.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo">sling:install</a></td>
<td>Install the project's artifact to a running Sling instance (in case it is an OSGi bundle). It uses the first valid OSGi bundle file for deployment from the primary artifact and all secondary ones. For details refer to <a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation">Bundle Installation</a>. To install an arbitrary bundle not attached to the current Maven project use goal <a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo">install-file</a>.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-file-mojo">sling:install-file</a></td>
<td>Install an OSGi bundle from a given file path or Maven coordinates (resolved from the repository) to a running Sling instance. One of the following parameter sets are used to determine the bundle to install (evaluated in the given order):
<ul>
<li><code>groupId</code>, <code>artifactId</code>, <code>version</code>, and optionally <code>classifier</code> and <code>packaging</code></li>
<li><code>artifact</code></li>
<li><code>bundleFileName</code></li>
</ul> To install a bundle which has been built from the current Maven project rather use goal <a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-install-mojo">install</a>. For details refer to <a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation">Bundle Installation</a>.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo">sling:uninstall</a></td>
<td>Uninstall an OSGi bundle from a running Sling instance. For details refer to <a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation">Bundle Uninstallation</a>.</td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo">sling:validate</a></td>
<td>Validate all JSON files of a project. It only ensures they are well-formed but not that they are valid against a certain schema.</td></tr></table>

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-plugin-info--system-requirements"></a>

## System Requirements

The following specifies the minimum requirements to run this Maven plugin:

Maven

3.8.1

JDK

11

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-plugin-info--system-requirements-history"></a>

## System Requirements History

The following specifies the minimum requirements to run this Maven plugin for historical versions:

| Plugin Version | Maven | JDK |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 3.0.4 | 3.8.1 | 11 |
| 3.0.2 | 3.3.1 | 8 |
| from 2.4.0 to 2.4.2 | 3.0.4 | 8 |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-plugin-info--usage"></a>

## Usage

You should specify the version in your project's plugin configuration:

```
<project>
  ...
  <build>
    <!-- To define the plugin version in your parent POM -->
    <pluginManagement>
      <plugins>
        <plugin>
          <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
          <artifactId>sling-maven-plugin</artifactId>
          <version>3.0.4</version>
        </plugin>
        ...
      </plugins>
    </pluginManagement>
    <!-- To use the plugin goals in your POM or parent POM -->
    <plugins>
      <plugin>
        <groupId>org.apache.sling</groupId>
        <artifactId>sling-maven-plugin</artifactId>
      </plugin>
      ...
    </plugins>
  </build>
  ...
</project>
```

For more information, see ["Guide to Configuring Plug-ins"](https://maven.apache.org/guides/mini/guide-configuring-plugins.html)

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-scm"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/scm.html -->

<!-- page_index: 269 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-scm--overview"></a>

# Overview

This project uses [Git](https://git-scm.com/) to manage its source code. Instructions on Git use can be found at <https://git-scm.com/doc>.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-scm--web-browser-access"></a>

# Web Browser Access

The following is a link to a browsable version of the source repository:

```
https://github.com/apache/sling-maven-plugin/tree/HEAD
```

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-scm--anonymous-access"></a>

# Anonymous Access

The source can be checked out anonymously from Git with this command (See <https://git-scm.com/docs/git-clone>):

```
$ git clone https://gitbox.apache.org/repos/asf/sling-maven-plugin.git
```

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-scm--developer-access"></a>

# Developer Access

Only project developers can access the Git tree via this method (See <https://git-scm.com/docs/git-clone>).

```
$ git clone https://gitbox.apache.org/repos/asf/sling-maven-plugin.git
```

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-scm--access-from-behind-a-firewall"></a>

# Access from Behind a Firewall

Refer to the documentation of the SCM used for more information about access behind a firewall.

<!-- page_index: 271 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-team--project-team"></a>

# Project Team

A successful project requires many people to play many roles. Some members write code or documentation, while others are valuable as testers, submitting patches and suggestions.

The project team is comprised of Members and Contributors. Members have direct access to the source of a project and actively evolve the code-base. Contributors improve the project through submission of patches and suggestions to the Members. The number of Contributors to the project is unbounded. Get involved today. All contributions to the project are greatly appreciated.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-team--members"></a>

## Members

The following is a list of developers with commit privileges that have directly contributed to the project in one way or another.

| Id | Name | URL |
| --- | --- | --- |
| sling | Apache Sling Project | <https://sling.apache.org/site/project-team.html> |

## Contributors

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/uninstall-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 272 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--sling:uninstall"></a>

# sling:uninstall

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:uninstall

**Description**:

Uninstall an OSGi bundle from a running Sling instance. For details refer to [Bundle Uninstallation](#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation).

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- The goal is not marked as thread-safe and thus does not support parallel builds.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<slingUrl>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--slingurl) | `URI` | `-` | The URL of the running Sling instance. The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment. For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets. **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console` **User Property**: `sling.url` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

<table class="table table-striped">
<tr>
<th>Name</th>
<th>Type</th>
<th>Since</th>
<th>Description</th></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--bundlefilename"><code>&lt;bundleFileName&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>File</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>
<div><strong>Deprecated.</strong>
Use <code>bundleName</code> instead</div>
The path of bundle file to uninstall. The file is only used to determine the file name or bundle symbolic name. This parameter is only effective if <code>bundleName</code> is not set.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.file</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--bundlename"><code>&lt;bundleName&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The bundles's file/resource name without path (for all <code>deploymentMethod</code>s except for <code>WebConsole</code>) or its symbolic name. If this parameter is set, it takes precedence over <code>bundleFileName</code>.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.bundle.name</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--bundlestart"><code>&lt;bundleStart&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Whether to start the uploaded bundle or not. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>true</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.bundle.start</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--bundlestartlevel"><code>&lt;bundleStartLevel&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The start level to set on the installed bundle. If the bundle is already installed and therefore is only updated this parameter is ignored. The parameter is also ignored if the running Sling instance has no StartLevel service (which is unusual actually). Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>20</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.bundle.startlevel</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--deploymentmethod"><code>&lt;deploymentMethod&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>BundleDeploymentMethod</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Bundle deployment method. One of the following three values are allowed
<ol>
<li><strong>WebConsole</strong>, uses the <a href="http://felix.apache.org/documentation/subprojects/apache-felix-web-console/web-console-restful-api.html#post-requests"> Felix Web Console REST API</a> for deployment (HTTP POST). This is the default. Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points to the Felix Web Console in that case.</li>
<li><strong>WebDAV</strong>, uses <a href="#documentation-development-repository-based-development"> WebDAV</a> for deployment (HTTP PUT). Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points to the entry path of the Sling WebDAV bundle (defaults to <code>/dav/default</code> in the Sling starter). Issues a HTTP Delete for the uninstall goal.</li>
<li><strong>SlingPostServlet</strong>, uses the <a href="#documentation-bundles-manipulating-content-the-slingpostservlet-servlets-post">Sling Post Servlet</a> for deployment (HTTP POST). Make sure that <code>slingUrl</code> points a path which is handled by the Sling POST Servlet (usually below regular Sling root URL).</li>
</ol> For more details refer to <a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation">Bundle Installation</a>. This has precedence over the deprecated parameter <code>usePut</code>. If nothing is set the default is either <code>WebConsole</code> or <code>WebDAV</code> (when <code>usePut</code> is <code>true</code>).
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.deploy.method</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--failonerror"><code>&lt;failOnError&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>true</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.failOnError</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"><code>&lt;httpConnectTimeoutSec&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>int</code></td>
<td><code>3.0.0</code></td>
<td>HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as <code>SSL</code> or <code>TLS</code> protocol negotiation).
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>10</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"><code>&lt;httpResponseTimeoutSec&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>int</code></td>
<td><code>3.0.0</code></td>
<td>HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>60</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--mimetype"><code>&lt;mimeType&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The content type / mime type used for WebDAV or Sling POST deployment.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>application/java-archive</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.mimeType</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--mountbyfs"><code>&lt;mountByFS&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Whether to add (for install)/remove (for uninstall) the mapping for the <a href="#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource">Apache Sling File System Resource Provider</a> for the <a href="#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading">bundle's initial content</a>. This parameter must not be <code>true</code> with bundles resolved from the Maven repository.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>false</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.mountByFS</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--password"><code>&lt;password&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>admin</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.password</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--refreshpackages"><code>&lt;refreshPackages&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>Whether to refresh the packages after installing the uploaded bundle. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>true</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.refreshPackages</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--slingconsoleurl"><code>&lt;slingConsoleUrl&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>URI</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.console.url</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--slingurlsuffix"><code>&lt;slingUrlSuffix&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>An optional url suffix which will be appended to the <code>sling.url</code> for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common <code>sling.url</code> in a parent POM (eg. <code>sling.url=http://localhost:8080</code> and <code>sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path</code>). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.urlSuffix</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--useput"><code>&lt;usePut&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>boolean</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>
<div><strong>Deprecated.</strong>
Use <code>deploymentMethod</code> instead.</div>
If a PUT via WebDAV should be used instead of the standard POST to the Felix Web Console. In the <code>uninstall</code> goal, a HTTP DELETE will be used.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>false</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.usePut</code></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><a href="#components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--user"><code>&lt;user&gt;</code></a></td>
<td><code>String</code></td>
<td><code>-</code></td>
<td>The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.
<strong>Default</strong>: <code>admin</code>
<strong>User Property</strong>: <code>sling.user</code></td></tr></table>

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--bundlefilename"></a>

### <bundleFileName>

**Deprecated.**
Use `bundleName` instead

The path of bundle file to uninstall. The file is only used to determine the file name or bundle symbolic name. This parameter is only effective if `bundleName` is not set.

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.file`
- **Default**: `${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}.jar`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--bundlename"></a>

### <bundleName>

The bundles's file/resource name without path (for all `deploymentMethod`s except for `WebConsole`) or its symbolic name. If this parameter is set, it takes precedence over `bundleFileName`.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.bundle.name`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--bundlestart"></a>

### <bundleStart>

Whether to start the uploaded bundle or not. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.bundle.start`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--bundlestartlevel"></a>

### <bundleStartLevel>

The start level to set on the installed bundle. If the bundle is already installed and therefore is only updated this parameter is ignored. The parameter is also ignored if the running Sling instance has no StartLevel service (which is unusual actually). Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.bundle.startlevel`
- **Default**: `20`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--deploymentmethod"></a>

### <deploymentMethod>

Bundle deployment method. One of the following three values are allowed

1. **WebConsole**, uses the  [Felix Web Console REST API](http://felix.apache.org/documentation/subprojects/apache-felix-web-console/web-console-restful-api.html#post-requests) for deployment (HTTP POST). This is the default. Make sure that `slingUrl` points to the Felix Web Console in that case.
2. **WebDAV**, uses  [WebDAV](#documentation-development-repository-based-development) for deployment (HTTP PUT). Make sure that `slingUrl` points to the entry path of the Sling WebDAV bundle (defaults to `/dav/default` in the Sling starter). Issues a HTTP Delete for the uninstall goal.
3. **SlingPostServlet**, uses the [Sling Post Servlet](#documentation-bundles-manipulating-content-the-slingpostservlet-servlets-post) for deployment (HTTP POST). Make sure that `slingUrl` points a path which is handled by the Sling POST Servlet (usually below regular Sling root URL).

For more details refer to [Bundle Installation](#components-sling-maven-plugin-bundle-installation). This has precedence over the deprecated parameter `usePut`. If nothing is set the default is either `WebConsole` or `WebDAV` (when `usePut` is `true`).

- **Type**: `org.apache.sling.maven.bundlesupport.deploy.BundleDeploymentMethod`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.deploy.method`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--failonerror"></a>

### <failOnError>

Determines whether or not to fail the build if the HTTP POST or PUT returns an non-OK response code.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.failOnError`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--httpconnecttimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpConnectTimeoutSec>

HTTP connection timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until a new connection is fully established. This may also include transport security negotiation exchanges such as `SSL` or `TLS` protocol negotiation).

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpConnectTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `10`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--httpresponsetimeoutsec"></a>

### <httpResponseTimeoutSec>

HTTP response timeout (in seconds). Determines the timeout until arrival of a response from the opposite endpoint.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Since**: `3.0.0`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.httpResponseTimeoutSec`
- **Default**: `60`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--mimetype"></a>

### <mimeType>

The content type / mime type used for WebDAV or Sling POST deployment.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.mimeType`
- **Default**: `application/java-archive`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--mountbyfs"></a>

### <mountByFS>

Whether to add (for install)/remove (for uninstall) the mapping for the [Apache Sling File System Resource Provider](#documentation-bundles-accessing-filesystem-resources-extensions-fsresource) for the [bundle's initial content](#documentation-bundles-content-loading-jcr-contentloader--initial-content-loading). This parameter must not be `true` with bundles resolved from the Maven repository.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.mountByFS`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--password"></a>

### <password>

The password to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.password`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--refreshpackages"></a>

### <refreshPackages>

Whether to refresh the packages after installing the uploaded bundle. Only applies when POSTing to Felix Web Console

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.refreshPackages`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--slingconsoleurl"></a>

### <slingConsoleUrl>

The WebConsole URL of the running Sling instance. This is required for file system provider operations. If not configured the value of slingUrl is used.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.console.url`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--slingurl"></a>

### <slingUrl>

The URL of the running Sling instance.

The default is only useful for **WebConsole** deployment.

For **WebDAV** deployment it is recommended to include the [Sling Simple WebDAV servlet root](#documentation-development-repository-based-development--separate-uri-space-webdav), for instance <http://localhost:8080/dav/default/libs/sling/install>. Omitting the `dav/default` segment can lead to conflicts with other servlets.

- **Type**: `java.net.URI`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **User Property**: `sling.url`
- **Default**: `http://localhost:8080/system/console`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--slingurlsuffix"></a>

### <slingUrlSuffix>

An optional url suffix which will be appended to the `sling.url` for use as the real target url. This allows to configure different target URLs in each POM, while using the same common `sling.url` in a parent POM (eg. `sling.url=http://localhost:8080` and `sling.urlSuffix=/project/specific/path`). This is typically used in conjunction with WebDAV or SlingPostServlet deployment methods.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.urlSuffix`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--useput"></a>

### <usePut>

**Deprecated.**
Use `deploymentMethod` instead.

If a PUT via WebDAV should be used instead of the standard POST to the Felix Web Console. In the `uninstall` goal, a HTTP DELETE will be used.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.usePut`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-uninstall-mojo--user"></a>

### <user>

The user name to authenticate at the running Sling instance.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.user`
- **Default**: `admin`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/sling-maven-plugin/validate-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 273 -->

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--sling:validate"></a>

# sling:validate

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:sling-maven-plugin:3.0.4:validate

**Description**:

Validate all JSON files of a project. It only ensures they are well-formed but not that they are valid against a certain schema.

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- The goal is thread-safe and supports parallel builds.
- Binds by default to the [lifecycle phase](https://maven.apache.org/ref/current/maven-core/lifecycles.html): `process-resources`.

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<jsonQuoteTick>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--jsonquotetick) | `boolean` | `-` | Whether to accept quote ticks in JSON files or not. **Default**: `false` **User Property**: `sling.validation.jsonQuoteTick` |
| [`<skip>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--skip) | `boolean` | `-` | Whether to skip the validation. **Default**: `false` **User Property**: `sling.validation.skip` |
| [`<skipJson>`](#components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--skipjson) | `boolean` | `-` | Whether to skip the json validation. At the time, there's no difference between `skip` and `skipJson` because only JSON files will be validated by now. **Default**: `false` **User Property**: `sling.validation.skipJson` |

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--jsonquotetick"></a>

### <jsonQuoteTick>

Whether to accept quote ticks in JSON files or not.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.validation.jsonQuoteTick`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--skip"></a>

### <skip>

Whether to skip the validation.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.validation.skip`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-sling-maven-plugin-validate-mojo--skipjson"></a>

### <skipJson>

Whether to skip the json validation. At the time, there's no difference between `skip` and `skipJson` because only JSON files will be validated by now.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `sling.validation.skipJson`
- **Default**: `false`

---

# Index of /components/slingfeature-maven-plugin-archives

<table>

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/slingfeature-maven-plugin/aggregate-features-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 279 -->

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--slingfeature:aggregate-features"></a>

# slingfeature:aggregate-features

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:slingfeature-maven-plugin:1.9.4:aggregate-features

**Description**:

Aggregate multiple features into a single one.

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- Requires dependency resolution of artifacts in scope: `test`.
- The goal is thread-safe and supports parallel builds.
- Binds by default to the [lifecycle phase](https://maven.apache.org/ref/current/maven-core/lifecycles.html): `generate-resources`.

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<aggregates>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--aggregates) | [`List<Aggregate>`](https://sling.apache.org/components/slingfeature-maven-plugin/apidocs/org/apache/sling/feature/maven/mojos/Aggregate.html) | `-` | The definition of the features used to create the new feature. |
| [`<features>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--features) | `File` | `-` | Directory containing feature files **Default**: `src/main/features` |
| [`<testFeatures>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--testfeatures) | `File` | `-` | Directory containing test feature files. **Default**: `src/test/features` |

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<additionalPostProcessHandlers>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--additionalpostprocesshandlers) | `List<String>` | `-` | Additional post process handlers to use when aggregating Normally handlers are discovered using the `java.util.ServiceLoader` mechanism but some special-purpose handlers are not registered by default. These handlers will be installed after the ones discovered by the `java.util.ServiceLoader`. |
| [`<defaultMetadata>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--defaultmetadata) | `Map<...>` | `-` | This parameter is only declared to make Maven happy, it is evaluated in the Preprossor |
| [`<enableLegacyVariableReplacement>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--enablelegacyvariablereplacement) | `boolean` | `1.3.6` | Enable old variable replacement in feature model based on the full maven project including system variables. If this is enabled, enableProjectVariableReplacement and replacePropertyVariables have no effect. **Default**: `false` |
| [`<enableProjectVariableReplacement>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--enableprojectvariablereplacement) | `boolean` | `1.3.6` | Enable the replacement of variables when reading a feature model. The supported variables are "project.groupId", "project.artifactId", "project.version" and "project.osgiVersion". **Default**: `true` |
| [`<featuresExcludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--featuresexcludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of excludes for the feature files. Feature files excluded by this configuration are not processed at all. |
| [`<featuresIncludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--featuresincludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of includes for the feature files in the configured directory. Only feature files specified by this include are processed. **Default**: `**/*.json` |
| [`<generatedFeatures>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--generatedfeatures) | `File` | `-` | Directory containing generated feature files |
| [`<generatedFeaturesExcludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--generatedfeaturesexcludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of excludes for the generated feature files. Feature files excluded by this configuration are not processed at all. |
| [`<generatedFeaturesIncludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--generatedfeaturesincludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of includes for the generated feature files in the configured directory. Only feature files specified by this include are processed. **Default**: `**/*.json` |
| [`<handlerConfiguration>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--handlerconfiguration) | `Map<String,Properties>` | `-` | No description. |
| [`<jarStartOrder>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--jarstartorder) | `int` | `-` | The start level for the attached jar/bundle. |
| [`<replacePropertyVariables>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--replacepropertyvariables) | `String` | `1.3.6` | A comma separated list of variables which are replaced when a feature model is read. The value of these variables is fetched from the project properties. |
| [`<skipAddFeatureDependencies>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--skipaddfeaturedependencies) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the artifacts from the feature are not added as dependencies with scope=`provided` to the project. **Default**: `false` |
| [`<skipAddJarToFeature>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--skipaddjartofeature) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the main jar artifact is not added to the feature. **Default**: `false` |
| [`<skipAddJarToTestFeature>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--skipaddjartotestfeature) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the main jar artifact is not added to the test feature. **Default**: `false` |
| [`<skipAddTestFeatureDependencies>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--skipaddtestfeaturedependencies) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the artifacts from the test feature are not added as dependencies with scope=`test` to the project. **Default**: `true` |
| [`<testFeaturesExcludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--testfeaturesexcludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of excludes for the test features. |
| [`<testFeaturesIncludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--testfeaturesincludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of includes for the test features. **Default**: `**/*.json` |
| [`<validateFeatures>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--validatefeatures) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the features are validated against the JSON schema. **Default**: `true` |

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--additionalpostprocesshandlers"></a>

### <additionalPostProcessHandlers>

Additional post process handlers to use when aggregating

Normally handlers are discovered using the `java.util.ServiceLoader` mechanism but some special-purpose handlers are not registered by default.

These handlers will be installed after the ones discovered by the `java.util.ServiceLoader`.

- **Type**: `java.util.List<java.lang.String>`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--aggregates"></a>

### <aggregates>

The definition of the features used to create the new feature.

- **Type**: [`java.util.List<org.apache.sling.feature.maven.mojos.Aggregate>`](https://sling.apache.org/components/slingfeature-maven-plugin/apidocs/org/apache/sling/feature/maven/mojos/Aggregate.html)
- **Required**: `Yes`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--defaultmetadata"></a>

### <defaultMetadata>

This parameter is only declared to make Maven happy, it is evaluated in the Preprossor

- **Type**: `java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.String>>`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--enablelegacyvariablereplacement"></a>

### <enableLegacyVariableReplacement>

Enable old variable replacement in feature model based on the full maven project including system variables. If this is enabled, enableProjectVariableReplacement and replacePropertyVariables have no effect.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Since**: `1.3.6`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--enableprojectvariablereplacement"></a>

### <enableProjectVariableReplacement>

Enable the replacement of variables when reading a feature model. The supported variables are "project.groupId", "project.artifactId", "project.version" and "project.osgiVersion".

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Since**: `1.3.6`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--features"></a>

### <features>

Directory containing feature files

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **Default**: `src/main/features`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--featuresexcludes"></a>

### <featuresExcludes>

Comma separated list of excludes for the feature files. Feature files excluded by this configuration are not processed at all.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--featuresincludes"></a>

### <featuresIncludes>

Comma separated list of includes for the feature files in the configured directory. Only feature files specified by this include are processed.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `**/*.json`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--generatedfeatures"></a>

### <generatedFeatures>

Directory containing generated feature files

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--generatedfeaturesexcludes"></a>

### <generatedFeaturesExcludes>

Comma separated list of excludes for the generated feature files. Feature files excluded by this configuration are not processed at all.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--generatedfeaturesincludes"></a>

### <generatedFeaturesIncludes>

Comma separated list of includes for the generated feature files in the configured directory. Only feature files specified by this include are processed.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `**/*.json`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--handlerconfiguration"></a>

### <handlerConfiguration>

No description.

- **Type**: `java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.util.Properties>`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--jarstartorder"></a>

### <jarStartOrder>

The start level for the attached jar/bundle.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--replacepropertyvariables"></a>

### <replacePropertyVariables>

A comma separated list of variables which are replaced when a feature model is read. The value of these variables is fetched from the project properties.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Since**: `1.3.6`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--skipaddfeaturedependencies"></a>

### <skipAddFeatureDependencies>

If set to `true` the artifacts from the feature are not added as dependencies with scope=`provided` to the project.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--skipaddjartofeature"></a>

### <skipAddJarToFeature>

If set to `true` the main jar artifact is not added to the feature.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--skipaddjartotestfeature"></a>

### <skipAddJarToTestFeature>

If set to `true` the main jar artifact is not added to the test feature.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--skipaddtestfeaturedependencies"></a>

### <skipAddTestFeatureDependencies>

If set to `true` the artifacts from the test feature are not added as dependencies with scope=`test` to the project.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--testfeatures"></a>

### <testFeatures>

Directory containing test feature files.

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **Default**: `src/test/features`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--testfeaturesexcludes"></a>

### <testFeaturesExcludes>

Comma separated list of excludes for the test features.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--testfeaturesincludes"></a>

### <testFeaturesIncludes>

Comma separated list of includes for the test features.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `**/*.json`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-aggregate-features-mojo--validatefeatures"></a>

### <validateFeatures>

If set to `true` the features are validated against the JSON schema.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://sling.apache.org/components/slingfeature-maven-plugin/analyse-features-mojo.html -->

<!-- page_index: 280 -->

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--slingfeature:analyse-features"></a>

# slingfeature:analyse-features

**Full name**:

org.apache.sling:slingfeature-maven-plugin:1.9.4:analyse-features

**Description**:

Analyse the feature.

**Attributes**:

- Requires a Maven project to be executed.
- Requires dependency resolution of artifacts in scope: `test`.
- The goal is thread-safe and supports parallel builds.
- Binds by default to the [lifecycle phase](https://maven.apache.org/ref/current/maven-core/lifecycles.html): `verify`.

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--required-parameters"></a>

## Required Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<features>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--features) | `File` | `-` | Directory containing feature files **Default**: `src/main/features` |
| [`<testFeatures>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--testfeatures) | `File` | `-` | Directory containing test feature files. **Default**: `src/test/features` |

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--optional-parameters"></a>

## Optional Parameters

| Name | Type | Since | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`<defaultMetadata>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--defaultmetadata) | `Map<...>` | `-` | This parameter is only declared to make Maven happy, it is evaluated in the Preprossor |
| [`<enableLegacyVariableReplacement>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--enablelegacyvariablereplacement) | `boolean` | `1.3.6` | Enable old variable replacement in feature model based on the full maven project including system variables. If this is enabled, enableProjectVariableReplacement and replacePropertyVariables have no effect. **Default**: `false` |
| [`<enableProjectVariableReplacement>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--enableprojectvariablereplacement) | `boolean` | `1.3.6` | Enable the replacement of variables when reading a feature model. The supported variables are "project.groupId", "project.artifactId", "project.version" and "project.osgiVersion". **Default**: `true` |
| [`<failOnAnalyserErrors>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--failonanalysererrors) | `boolean` | `-` | No description. **Default**: `true` **User Property**: `failon.analyser.errors` |
| [`<featuresExcludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--featuresexcludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of excludes for the feature files. Feature files excluded by this configuration are not processed at all. |
| [`<featuresIncludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--featuresincludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of includes for the feature files in the configured directory. Only feature files specified by this include are processed. **Default**: `**/*.json` |
| [`<framework>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--framework) | `Dependency` | `-` | The framework to use for analysing the feature models if not contained in an execution-environment extension. |
| [`<generatedFeatures>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--generatedfeatures) | `File` | `-` | Directory containing generated feature files |
| [`<generatedFeaturesExcludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--generatedfeaturesexcludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of excludes for the generated feature files. Feature files excluded by this configuration are not processed at all. |
| [`<generatedFeaturesIncludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--generatedfeaturesincludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of includes for the generated feature files in the configured directory. Only feature files specified by this include are processed. **Default**: `**/*.json` |
| [`<jarStartOrder>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--jarstartorder) | `int` | `-` | The start level for the attached jar/bundle. |
| [`<logWarnings>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--logwarnings) | `boolean` | `1.3.8` | By default the warnings from the analysers are logged. Setting this to false disables logging them. **Default**: `true` |
| [`<replacePropertyVariables>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--replacepropertyvariables) | `String` | `1.3.6` | A comma separated list of variables which are replaced when a feature model is read. The value of these variables is fetched from the project properties. |
| [`<scans>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--scans) | [`List<Scan>`](https://sling.apache.org/components/slingfeature-maven-plugin/apidocs/org/apache/sling/feature/maven/mojos/Scan.html) | `-` | The scans for the analyser |
| [`<skipAddFeatureDependencies>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--skipaddfeaturedependencies) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the artifacts from the feature are not added as dependencies with scope=`provided` to the project. **Default**: `false` |
| [`<skipAddJarToFeature>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--skipaddjartofeature) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the main jar artifact is not added to the feature. **Default**: `false` |
| [`<skipAddJarToTestFeature>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--skipaddjartotestfeature) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the main jar artifact is not added to the test feature. **Default**: `false` |
| [`<skipAddTestFeatureDependencies>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--skipaddtestfeaturedependencies) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the artifacts from the test feature are not added as dependencies with scope=`test` to the project. **Default**: `true` |
| [`<testFeaturesExcludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--testfeaturesexcludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of excludes for the test features. |
| [`<testFeaturesIncludes>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--testfeaturesincludes) | `String` | `-` | Comma separated list of includes for the test features. **Default**: `**/*.json` |
| [`<validateFeatures>`](#components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--validatefeatures) | `boolean` | `-` | If set to `true` the features are validated against the JSON schema. **Default**: `true` |

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--parameter-details"></a>

## Parameter Details

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--defaultmetadata"></a>

### <defaultMetadata>

This parameter is only declared to make Maven happy, it is evaluated in the Preprossor

- **Type**: `java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.String>>`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--enablelegacyvariablereplacement"></a>

### <enableLegacyVariableReplacement>

Enable old variable replacement in feature model based on the full maven project including system variables. If this is enabled, enableProjectVariableReplacement and replacePropertyVariables have no effect.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Since**: `1.3.6`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `false`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--enableprojectvariablereplacement"></a>

### <enableProjectVariableReplacement>

Enable the replacement of variables when reading a feature model. The supported variables are "project.groupId", "project.artifactId", "project.version" and "project.osgiVersion".

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Since**: `1.3.6`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--failonanalysererrors"></a>

### <failOnAnalyserErrors>

No description.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Required**: `No`
- **User Property**: `failon.analyser.errors`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--features"></a>

### <features>

Directory containing feature files

- **Type**: `java.io.File`
- **Required**: `Yes`
- **Default**: `src/main/features`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--featuresexcludes"></a>

### <featuresExcludes>

Comma separated list of excludes for the feature files. Feature files excluded by this configuration are not processed at all.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--featuresincludes"></a>

### <featuresIncludes>

Comma separated list of includes for the feature files in the configured directory. Only feature files specified by this include are processed.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `**/*.json`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--framework"></a>

The framework to use for analysing the feature models if not contained in an execution-environment extension.

- **Type**: `org.apache.maven.model.Dependency`
- **Required**: `No`

---

- **Required**: `No`

---

Comma separated list of excludes for the generated feature files. Feature files excluded by this configuration are not processed at all.

- **Type**: `java.lang.String`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `**/*.json`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--jarstartorder"></a>

### <jarStartOrder>

The start level for the attached jar/bundle.

- **Type**: `int`
- **Required**: `No`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--logwarnings"></a>

### <logWarnings>

By default the warnings from the analysers are logged. Setting this to false disables logging them.

- **Type**: `boolean`
- **Since**: `1.3.8`
- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `true`

---

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-analyse-features-mojo--replacepropertyvariables"></a>

A comma separated list of variables which are replaced when a feature model is read. The value of these variables is fetched from the project properties.

### <skipAddFeatureDependencies>

If set to `true` the artifacts from the feature are not added as dependencies with scope=`provided` to the project.

- **Required**: `No`
- **Default**: `false`

<a id="components-slingfeature-maven-plugin-apis-jar-mojo"></a>
